_meta.keyKnowledge: We design algorithms with nested control structures, represent them as flowcharts and pseudocode, and use tracing to check they work.

_source.w: We design algorithms with nested control structures, represent them as flowcharts and pseudocode, and use tracing to check they work.
y: Nested control structures allow us to solve increasingly complex and nuanced problems.
Formal representation using flowcharts and pseudocode is standard practice in software development.
Tracing algorithms manually develops our ability to think precisely and debug effectively.
eg: We design algorithms with nested loops and conditional statements for complex tasks.
We represent our algorithms using standard flowchart symbols and clear pseudocode.
We trace through our algorithms with sample inputs to verify they produce correct outputs.
We identify and fix logical errors by stepping through our algorithm carefully.
We compare the efficiency of different algorithmic approaches to the same problem.
We document our algorithms with clear comments explaining each section's purpose.
